result.summary: Mapped Auth0’s public developer integration surface: 4 HTTP APIs (Authentication, Management, My Account, My Organization), 1 CLI (auth0), and 1 MCP server package; all require Auth0-issued credentials via user tokens, M2M app credentials, CLI login, or MCP device-flow login.

auth0_m2m_app.label: Auth0 machine-to-machine application credentials
auth0_m2m_app.setup: In the [Auth0 Dashboard Applications page](https://manage.auth0.com/#/applications), create a Machine to Machine application (or use an existing confidential application), authorize it for the target API, and copy its `client_id` and `client_secret`. For the Management API, request a token with audience `https://{yourDomain}/api/v2/` as described in [Management API Access Tokens](https://auth0.com/docs/secure/tokens/access-tokens/management-api-access-tokens). For the My Organization API, first [activate the API](https://auth0.com/docs/api/myorganization) and then grant the application the needed scopes.

auth0_user_access_token.label: Auth0 user access token
auth0_user_access_token.setup: Create or configure an Auth0 application in the [Auth0 Dashboard Applications page](https://manage.auth0.com/#/applications), then obtain a user access token through an Auth0-supported user flow such as Authorization Code or PKCE against your tenant domain. Set the API audience to the target API, for example `https://{yourDomain}/me/` for My Account API, as documented in [My Account API](https://auth0.com/docs/api/myaccount) and [Get Token](https://auth0.com/docs/api/authentication/authorization-code-flow/get-token).

auth0_client_id_secret.label: Auth0 application client ID and client secret
auth0_client_id_secret.setup: Create or open a confidential application in the [Auth0 Dashboard Applications page](https://manage.auth0.com/#/applications) and copy its `client_id` and `client_secret`. The Authentication API accepts these either in the request body or via HTTP `Basic` auth depending on the application's token endpoint authentication method, as documented in [Authentication API](https://auth0.com/docs/api/authentication).

auth0_private_key_jwt.label: Auth0 application client ID with private-key JWT assertion
auth0_private_key_jwt.setup: Create or open a confidential application in the [Auth0 Dashboard Applications page](https://manage.auth0.com/#/applications), configure token endpoint authentication for private key JWT, and use its `client_id` plus your private signing key to generate a `client_assertion` JWT as described in [Authentication API](https://auth0.com/docs/api/authentication).

auth0_public_client_id.label: Auth0 public application client ID
auth0_public_client_id.setup: Create or open a public application (for example SPA or native app) in the [Auth0 Dashboard Applications page](https://manage.auth0.com/#/applications) and copy its `client_id`. Some Authentication API endpoints accept only the `client_id` for public apps, as documented in [Authentication API](https://auth0.com/docs/api/authentication).

auth0_cli_login.label: Auth0 CLI login
auth0_cli_login.setup: Install the [Auth0 CLI](https://auth0.com/docs/deploy-monitor/auth0-cli) and run `auth0 login` for browser-based login, or `auth0 login --domain <your-domain> --client-id <client-id> --client-secret <client-secret>` to authenticate with a machine-to-machine application. The CLI stores credentials locally after login.

variables.name: yourDomain
variables.description: Auth0 tenant domain shown in docs as `{yourDomain}`.

variables.name: yourDomain
variables.resolveFrom: your Auth0 tenant domain or custom domain
variables.description: Use the same Auth0 or custom domain consistently for token issuance, audience, and API calls.

variables.name: tenantDomain
variables.description: Auth0 tenant domain, for example `{TENANT}.auth0.com` or a configured custom domain where supported.

variables.name: tenantDomain
variables.resolveFrom: your Auth0 tenant domain or custom domain
variables.description: Use the same Auth0 or custom domain consistently for token issuance, audience, and My Organization API calls.
